Package org.eventb.internal.pp.core.elements

Class Literal<S extends Literal<S,T>,T extends Term>

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.internal.pp.core.elements.Hashable
org.eventb.internal.pp.core.elements.Literal<S,T>
Type Parameters:
S - subclasses should put themselves as a parameter. Used to implement true covariance.
T - type of terms contained in this literal
Direct Known Subclasses:
ArithmeticLiteral, EqualityLiteral, PredicateLiteral
public abstract class Literal<S extends Literal<S,T>,T extends Term> extends Hashable
Abstract base class for literals.

There are three types of literals, PredicateLiteral, EqualityLiteral and ArithmeticLiteral.

Instances of this class are immutable and all accessor methods return an immutable object or a shallow copy of a list of immutable objects.

  • Method Details

    • getTerms

      public List<T> getTerms()
      Returns the terms of this literal.
      Returns:
      the terms of this literal

    • getTerm

      public T getTerm(int index)
      Returns the term at the specified index.
      Parameters:
      index - the index
      Returns:
      the term at the specified index

    • getTermsSize

      public int getTermsSize()
      Returns the number of terms of this literal.
      Returns:
      the number of terms of this literal

    • isQuantified

      public boolean isQuantified()
      Returns true if this literal is quantified, false otherwise.

      A literal is quantified iff one of the term contains an instance of LocalVariable.

      Returns:
      true if this literal is quantified, false otherwise

    • isConstant

      public boolean isConstant()
      Returns true if this literal is constant and false otherwise.

      A literal is constant if none of its terms contain an instance of Variable.

      Returns:
      true if this literal is constant and false otherwise

    • isForall

      public boolean isForall()
      Returns true if this literal is quantified and the quantifier is a forall quantifier.
      Returns:
      true if this literal is quantified and the quantifier is a forall quantifier, false otherwise

    • getCopyWithNewVariables

      public S getCopyWithNewVariables(VariableContext context, HashMap<SimpleTerm,SimpleTerm> substitutionsMap)
      Returns a copy of this literal where all variables and local variables have been replaced by a fresh instance.

      It holds that literal.getCopyWithNewVariables(...).equalsWithDifferentVariables( literal)

      Parameters:
      context - the variable context
      substitutionsMap - the substitution map
      Returns:
      a copy of this literal with new instances of variables.

    • collectLocalVariables

      public void collectLocalVariables(Set<LocalVariable> variables)
      Puts all local variables that occur in this literal in the specified set.
      Parameters:
      variables - the set of local variables to populate

    • collectVariables

      public void collectVariables(Set<Variable> variables)
      Puts all variables that occur in this literal in the specified set.
      Parameters:
      variables - the set of variables to populate

    • substitute

      public abstract S substitute(Map<SimpleTerm,SimpleTerm> map)
      Returns a copy of this literal where the substitution given in the specified map is applied.
      Parameters:
      map - the substitution map
      Returns:
      a copy of this literal with the applied substitution

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object obj)
      Overrides:
      equals in class Object

    • equalsWithDifferentVariables

      public boolean equalsWithDifferentVariables(S literal, HashMap<SimpleTerm,SimpleTerm> map)
      Return true if this literal is equal to the specified literal but with different instances of variables.

      Parameters:
      literal - the literal to be checked for equality
      map - the map of correspondance for variables
      Returns:
      true if this literal is equal to the specified literal but with different instances of variables, false otherwise
      See Also:
      • Term.equalsWithDifferentVariables(Term, HashMap)

    • getInverse

      public abstract S getInverse()
      Returns a copy of this literal with the opposite sign.
      Returns:
      a copy of this literal with the opposite sign
